pending_diagnosis: DIAGNOSIS: Sporadic Creutzfeldt-Jakob Disease (sCJD) THE SMOKING GUN: Positive RT-QuIC (Real-Time Quaking-Induced Conversion) assay on the cerebrospinal fluid (CSF). (This test confirms the presence of the pathogenic prion protein, PrPSc, offering definitive ante-mortem diagnosis faster than biopsy). PATHOPHYSIOLOGY: CJD belongs to the family of Transmissible Spongiform Encephalopathies (TSEs) caused by prions—misfolded proteins lacking nucleic acid. In the sporadic form (sCJD), the normal cellular prion protein (PrPC) spontaneously converts into the pathogenic isoform (PrPSc). This misfolded protein is highly resistant to degradation and acts as a template, rapidly converting surrounding normal proteins into the pathologic form. The accumulation of PrPSc causes vacuolation (spongiform change) of the gray matter, leading to rapid neuronal death. The classic triad includes rapidly progressive dementia, myoclonus, and characteristic EEG (PSWCs) and MRI findings (cortical ribboning and basal ganglia hyperintensities). The condition is universally and rapidly fatal, typically within 6 to 12 months from symptom onset.
